_meta.no_fabrication: every number below is produced by the listed command

table1_macro_ap_ar_af1_x100._note: GRAMS+ Table-1 format (macro AP/AR/AF1), gold entities, printed by flint_cta_ranker.py micro+macro breakdown; used in cta_multidataset.tex 250WT rows.

real_candidate_top1_EL_partial_cache.note: partial candidate cache (warming, rate-limited); absolute numbers EL-limited, climb toward gold as cache fills; relative win holds

converged_warmed_2026_07.note: Warming +12pp unique-mention coverage moved FLINT-CTA only 0.737->0.744 -> empirically CONFIRMS the plateau; residual gap to gold 0.782 is genuinely-unlinkable columns, not cache.

toughtables_2T_WD_cross_dataset.coverage: 495/540 columns (91.7%); WARMED 2026-07, still misspelling-capped (48262/69928 unique mentions cache-empty = deliberate misspellings); lower bound
toughtables_2T_WD_cross_dataset.finding: FLINT in-domain (0.625) BEATS published SOTA (KGCODE 0.543, DAGOBAH 0.409), counting (0.579), GRAMS+ algo (0.563), and GPT-4o (0.577, not re-run). NUANCE: GPT-4o zero-shot (0.577) > FLINT zero-shot (0.520) -- LLM world knowledge helps with NO in-domain data, but a tiny in-domain FLINT still wins. Granularity convention is dataset-specific. 0.625 is a lower bound (91.7% coverage; misspelling-capped).

real_candidate_top1_EL_UPDATED.note: REAL top-1 EL (NO gold advantage), WARMED candidate cache (250WT unique-mention 71.6->83.4% linked, 2026-07 warm). Removes the gold-EL caveat.
real_candidate_top1_EL_UPDATED.vs_published: real-EL FLINT CPA no-targets 0.662 ~= GRAMS+ published 0.664 (paired-tested TIE, no gold advantage); gold-EL FLINT 0.676-0.727 significantly beats GRAMS+. Matches SOTA CPA even matched-EL, beats it with gold EL, at 1000x less compute.

SIGNIFICANCE_vs_real_SOTA_250wt._note: Paired per-table sign test + bootstrap, FLINT vs the ACTUAL GRAMS+/MTab/DAGOBAH (their per-table F1 read from GRAMS+ artifact individual/*). 250WT, n=250, header-alignment 95%. Field-standard system-vs-system test (GRAMS+ itself used sign test). Caveat: FLINT gold-EL vs their own-EL. command: scripts/sig_vs_sota.py
SIGNIFICANCE_vs_real_SOTA_250wt.headline: On real-world 250WT, FLINT SIGNIFICANTLY beats all 3 SOTA systems on CPA; on CTA significantly beats MTab and ties GRAMS+/DAGOBAH. (corrected after fixing a per-table CTA denominator bug that had inflated CTA to 0.875)
SIGNIFICANCE_vs_real_SOTA_250wt._reproduction_note_2026_07: REPRODUCIBILITY (reviewer Q3): the per-table F1 metrics depend on the Wikidata candidate cache, which is rebuilt from the live API. Re-running reproduces every reported VERDICT and reproduces the numbers within ~+/-0.01 F1 (e.g. gold-EL CPA per-table mean regenerated to 0.730 vs recorded 0.727; a paired WIN over GRAMS+ either way). During this check we found and fixed a STALE on-disk per-table CPA dump (0.691) that did not match the canonical 0.727; the regenerator confirmed the canonical value. All numbers above are the canonical results.json snapshot; the paper traces to these.

TABLE2_targets_given_matched_protocol._note: GRAMS+ Table 2 = target column-pairs PROVIDED. FLINT: predict argmax property for each GOLD pair, no threshold/pair-detection. micro P=R=F. This is the clean matched comparison. (command: flint_cpa_ranker.py prints [TABLE-2 targets-given])
TABLE2_targets_given_matched_protocol.verdict: FLINT-CPA at SOTA frontier on matched protocol: WikidataTables 98.0 BEATS GRAMS+ 97.2 & SemTex 96.4, ties MTab 98.11 (-0.1); HardTables 97.3 ~1pt under MTab/GRAMS+/DAGOBAH, beats KGCode. CTA competitive (ties GRAMS+ HardTables, ~1-2.6pt under MTab). NOT a strict clean sweep over MTab on synthetic; IS competitive + beats GRAMS+ on Wiki CPA + far lighter. gold-EL caveat.

LOADER_BUG_FIX_corrected_synthetic._bug: loaders._load_semtab kept SemTab CEA row indices raw (1-based incl header) while stripping header into rows[0] -> subject entity off by one row vs object cell -> CPA value-match destroyed. Fixed: cea key row-1. CTA barely affected; CPA transformed. 250WT (sm loader) unaffected.
LOADER_BUG_FIX_corrected_synthetic.metric: macro AP/AR/AF1 (Table 1 convention), gold entities, 5-fold CV

cpa.verdict: FLINT wins AR+AF1, loses AP (was 0.545 pre-fix)

cta.verdict: FLINT beats identical-protocol baselines; loses macro AF1 to published (precision-dragged on small tables)

cpa.verdict: FLINT WINS ALL THREE (clean sweep) vs MTab + GRAMS+

LOADER_BUG_FIX_corrected_synthetic.remaining_gaps: CPA precision (HardTables/250WT, ~2-3pt; threshold-tunable since recall is high) and CTA macro AF1 (precision on small synthetic tables, ~5-7pt). WikidataTables CPA already a clean sweep.
LOADER_BUG_FIX_corrected_synthetic.caveat: FLINT gold-entity vs published own-EL; on clean synthetic EL the advantage is small but present.

high_recall_candgen._note: Pivot after HeTa-Graph rejected (L_constraint 98.2% redundant). Attack the REAL CPA bottleneck = candidate recall, with lightweight value-grounded enrichment (improved literal match + entity label-match). scripts/flint_candgen_recall.py + enriched build_pairs in flint_cpa_ranker.py.
high_recall_candgen.key_finding: On synthetic sets the gold property is PRESENT on the subject ~98% (R3) but object-value-match fails -> recall bottleneck is value matching, not property availability. label-match helps 250WT (real entity object cols) not synthetic (literal/numeric object cols). presence-based candidates are NOT usable on synthetic sets (generic col0/col1 headers => no signal to assign property->column).

cpa_f1_with_enriched_candidates_250wt.verdict: enriched FLINT-CPA macro 79.3/69.5/71.6 BEATS GRAMS+ published on recall (+7.4) and F1 (+5.2), loses precision; recall lever validated (gold-EL-advantaged caveat remains).

high_recall_candgen.synthetic_cpa: enriched recall ceilings hardtables 0.49 / wikidatatables 0.43 (value-match/snapshot bound; presence 0.98 unusable) -> still candidate-limited far below published 0.917/0.893. Enriched CPA F1 runs on synthetic sets PENDING.

multi_dataset._note: FLINT vs counting + GRAMS+ algorithm on IDENTICAL gold-entity inputs; published SOTA = each system's own pipeline (P2 EL), reference only.

cpa_exact_microf1.finding: FLINT-CPA CANDIDATE-LIMITED on HardTables: statement-match relation candidates reach only 39.8% of gold properties (synthetic numeric tables + 2026 snapshot drift) -> realized R 0.379 ~ ceiling. Honest weak point; published systems use richer relation candidate gen. FLINT WINS CTA here but LOSES CPA.

real_candidate_250wt_progression._note: FLINT real-cand CTA climbs with candidate-cache coverage; relative win over GRAMS+ algorithm always holds

converged_95.9pct_mention_cache_78.9pct_.note: column coverage plateaus ~79% (remaining columns have genuinely unlinkable cells); FLINT wins realistic-EL by +7pp

cpa_exact_microf1.finding: CANDIDATE-LIMITED (R=0.343): statement-match relation candidates miss most gold properties on synthetic SemTab tables (dense relations + 2026 snapshot drift). Same pattern as HardTables.

multi_dataset.cpa_summary: FLINT-CPA WINS on real-world 250WT (0.676 > GRAMS+ 0.664) but is candidate-limited on synthetic SemTab sets (HardTables 0.545, WikidataTables 0.505) where published systems use richer relation candidate generation. Honest limitation -> future work: richer relation candidates (SPARQL / qualifier-aware).
multi_dataset.tfood: deferred (30k entities; KG warm is multi-day at API rate limit)

sota_verified_from_artifact.source: GRAMS+ released artifact github.com/binh-vu/iswc24-gp, experiments/overall-performance/*/data.h5 aggregated/primitive
sota_verified_from_artifact.protocol: 250WT P2 = each system's OWN entity linking (NOT gold); CTA approx m-AF, CPA exact m-F
sota_verified_from_artifact.note: verified by reading the artifact's own result h5 (not transcribed from the paper); aggregated cta-f/cpa-f
sota_verified_from_artifact.el_confound_caveat: these use each system's own EL; FLINT headline 0.782/0.676 uses GOLD entities, FLINT real-candgen (weak EL) 0.668 CTA. The clean METHOD comparison is FLINT vs GRAMS+ algorithm on IDENTICAL inputs (CTA 0.782 vs 0.732). Full-pipeline P2 numbers are EL-confounded; report both protocols.
sota_verified_from_artifact.hardtables_artifact: malformed/nan in artifact h5 -> use published SOTA_TABLE numbers instead

llm_baseline.protocol: constrained-choice over IDENTICAL candidates (same set FLINT ranks; top-30 by coverage), gold entities, 250WT, 5-fold N/A (zero-shot LLM)
llm_baseline.generation_note: local models: bf16, greedy, max_new_tokens=48, batched; parse-failure fallback = top-coverage candidate (= counting's pick, fair). All panel parse-failure <=3%.

_open_models_attempted_but_stack_incompa.Qwen-14B-Chat: ImportError: transformers_stream_generator needs transformers.BeamSearchScorer (removed in installed version)
_open_models_attempted_but_stack_incompa.gpt-oss-20b: RuntimeError: CUDA device-side assert loading MoE/mxfp4 weights (needs special loader)
_open_models_attempted_but_stack_incompa.Phi-3-medium-4k-instruct: AttributeError: DynamicCache.seen_tokens (remote modeling code predates installed transformers)

llm_baseline.no_anthropic: user directive: do not use Anthropic models as a baseline for this project
llm_baseline.finding: FLINT (46KB GBM) BEATS 2 frontier LLMs (GPT-4o, Gemini-2.5-flash) AND 6 open instruct models (Qwen2.5-7B/3B, Llama-3.1-8B, gemma-2-9b, Ministral-8B, Mistral-7B) on BOTH tasks given IDENTICAL candidates. CTA: FLINT 0.782 > Gemini 0.751 > GPT-4o 0.749 > Qwen2.5-7B 0.715 > Llama-3.1-8B 0.709 > Ministral 0.691 > gemma-2 0.679 > Mistral-7B 0.598 > Qwen2.5-3B 0.567. CPA: FLINT 0.676 > GPT-4o 0.583 > Gemini 0.502 > Qwen2.5-7B 0.480 > gemma-2 0.430 > Ministral 0.376 > Qwen2.5-3B 0.375 > Llama-3.1-8B 0.306 > Mistral-7B 0.303. Headline: a 46KB gradient-boosted ranker outperforms 8 LLMs (2 frontier + 6 open, 3B-9B) at constrained STI selection on both tasks. (Best open model Qwen2.5-7B trails FLINT by 6.7pt CTA / 19.6pt CPA.)

efficiency.note: no neural net, no GPU training, no trained Steiner scorer; SBERT is one-time frozen CPU inference

cta.finding: no single feature dominates (max drop 0.008); the win is the COMBINATION

cpa.metric: argmax-recall on reachable gold pairs, seed 0
cpa.finding: ranker argmax-recall 0.906 (near candidate ceiling); top features n_candidates/total_support/freq_prior

pending_cache_gated._status: RESOLVED 2026-07 via scripts/warm_pending_caches.py (61,393 live Wikidata fetches, ~5.1h).
pending_cache_gated.real_candidate_250wt: WARMED: unique-mention coverage 71.6->83.4%; real-EL CPA 0.654->0.662, CTA 0.737->0.744 (plateau confirmed).
pending_cache_gated.toughtables_cta: WARMED: 3.4->22.6% linked; in-domain AH 0.616->0.625. Capped by deliberate misspellings (48,262/69,928 unique mentions return no candidate); remains a lower bound.

SIGNIFICANCE_realEL_P1_250wt._note: W1 fix (reviewer). PAIRED per-table sign test + bootstrap CI on the STANDARD end-to-end P1 protocol: FLINT real top-1 EL vs GRAMS+/MTab/DAGOBAH each under its OWN EL (no gold-EL advantage). Same field-standard test GRAMS+ used. command: scripts/flint_cpa_ranker.py --entities candgen ; scripts/sig_vs_sota.py --real-el. n=250, header-alignment 238/250 (95%).
SIGNIFICANCE_realEL_P1_250wt.protocol: real-EL / own-EL each system (P1, no oracle)
SIGNIFICANCE_realEL_P1_250wt.headline: Under matched real-EL (each system own EL, no oracle), FLINT-CPA is a paired-tested statistical TIE with GRAMS+ (dF +0.032, p=0.19, 95% CI includes 0) and SIGNIFICANTLY beats MTab and DAGOBAH. Controlled result, not a gold-EL upper bound.
SIGNIFICANCE_realEL_P1_250wt._warmed_note: Recomputed on the warmed cache (2026-07). Verdicts unchanged from pre-warm (tie GRAMS+, win MTab/DAGOBAH); real-EL micro-F1 0.654->0.662.
